text.title: Django Celery Tasks Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Django Celery Tasks application must enforce. These are HARD rules — implementation expedience does not override them.

rule.statement: Every @shared_task or @app.task carries an explicit `name='app.module.task'` argument; never rely on auto-discovered names that change when modules move.
rule.rationale: Auto-discovered names break silently on module rename; queued jobs deserialize against missing tasks and dead-letter.

rule.statement: Every task has an idempotency strategy: idempotency key in payload, DB upsert by natural key, or 'check before act' pattern. Retries must be safe.
rule.rationale: Celery delivers at-least-once; tasks that mutate state without idempotency double-bill, double-email, or corrupt counters.

rule.statement: Tasks declare max_retries, retry_backoff (exponential), retry_jitter (true), and retry_kwargs. Never rely on Celery defaults silently.
rule.rationale: Default retry policy retries forever or never depending on version; explicit policy keeps behavior reviewable.

rule.statement: Every task sets both soft_time_limit and time_limit. soft raises an exception inside the task; time_limit kills the worker process.
rule.rationale: Without time limits, a hung task pins a worker indefinitely; the pool starves and unrelated tasks back up.

rule.statement: Tasks declare an explicit queue via `@shared_task(queue='emails')`. Workers consume specific queues; never run a worker on default-only.
rule.rationale: All-tasks-on-default mixes priorities; a slow task on default starves time-sensitive tasks.

rule.statement: Tasks that exhaust retries route to a dead-letter queue or store a failure record with structured fields. An alert fires within minutes.
rule.rationale: Silent terminal failures accumulate invisibly; alerting + DLQ give operators a single place to triage.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring done.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The run leaf maps "preconditions met" to the canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The skip leaf needs a real rule so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.
